document_title: TCG TPM 2.0 Library V1.85 RC4 — Part 0: Introduction
short_description: Overview and change summary for the TCG TPM 2.0 Library V1.85 RC4 specification. Introduces PQC algorithm IDs (TPM_ALG_MLKEM=0x00A0; TPM_ALG_MLDSA=0x00A1) and explains the rationale for enlarging TPM_BUFFER_MAX from 4096 to 8192 bytes to accommodate ML-DSA-87 signatures.
applicable_industries: Hardware Security; IoT; Embedded Systems; Enterprise

document_title: TCG TPM 2.0 Library V1.85 RC4 — Part 1: Architecture
short_description: Defines the TPM 2.0 architecture including the four-hierarchy model (Platform/Endorsement/Storage/Owner) and their handle values (TPM_RH_ENDORSEMENT=0x4000000B; TPM_RH_OWNER=0x40000001; TPM_RH_PLATFORM=0x4000000C). Specifies PQC key roles (EK; SRK; AIK; IDevID) and the impact of ML-KEM-768 and ML-DSA-65 adoption on attestation flows and TPM_BUFFER_MAX.
applicable_industries: Hardware Security; IoT; Embedded Systems; Enterprise
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TPM key hierarchy; Attestation; Secure boot
ToolchainSupport: TPM manufacturers; Platform vendors; Device identity

document_title: TCG TPM 2.0 Library V1.85 RC4 — Part 2: Structures
short_description: Normative structure definitions for TPM 2.0 V1.85. Assigns TPM_ALG_MLKEM=0x00A0 and TPM_ALG_MLDSA=0x00A1. Defines TPMT_ASYM_SCHEME for ML-KEM encapsulation and ML-DSA signing. Specifies key-size constants: ML-KEM-768 pk=1184B/ct=1088B; ML-DSA-65 pk=1952B/sig=3309B. Sets TPM_BUFFER_MAX=8192 and TPM2B_MAX_BUFFER size.
applicable_industries: Hardware Security; IoT; Embedded Systems; Enterprise
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TPM wire protocol structures; Key size budgeting

document_title: TCG TPM 2.0 Library V1.85 RC4 — Part 3: Commands
short_description: Command specification for TPM 2.0 V1.85. Defines TPM2_CreatePrimary for PQC key roles using ML-KEM-768 (EK/SRK; TPM_ALG_MLKEM=0x00A0) and ML-DSA-65 (AIK/IDevID; TPM_ALG_MLDSA=0x00A1). Specifies TPM2_Quote behaviour with ML-DSA signatures and TPM2_EncryptDecap for KEM-based credential activation replacing classical RSA-OAEP.
applicable_industries: Hardware Security; IoT; Embedded Systems; Enterprise
ToolchainSupport: TPM manufacturers; Firmware engineers; Protocol implementers

document_title: Importing External Pre-Shared Keys (PSKs) for TLS 1.3
short_description: RFC 9258 defines an external PSK importer interface for TLS 1.3 (RFC 8446). It allows QKD-derived keys delivered via ETSI GS QKD 014 to be bound to a specific KDF and hash algorithm and imported as a TLS 1.3 pre-shared key — enabling QKD integration without any TLS protocol modifications.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: TLS 1.3 PSK; QKD key injection into TLS handshake

document_title: Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions (Revision 1)
short_description: NIST SP 800-108 Rev.1 specifies key derivation functions (KDFs) based on pseudorandom functions including counter mode feedback mode and double-pipeline mode using HMAC or CMAC. Directly implemented in PKCS#11 v3.0 as CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF and used in HSM-based QKD key derivation pipelines.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM key derivation from QKD secret; PKCS#11 CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF
ToolchainSupport: HSM vendors (Thales; Utimaco; nCipher); OpenSSL KBKDF

document_title: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
short_description: NIST SP 800-56C Rev.2 specifies one-step and extraction-then-expansion (EtE) key derivation methods for key-establishment schemes. The EtE approach supports hybrid shared secrets combining classical DH or PQC KEM output with a QKD-sourced entropy value — directly applicable to quantum-hybrid key exchange architectures.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Hybrid key derivation (classical + QKD); TLS 1.3 key schedule

document_title: PKCS #11 Cryptographic Token Interface Standard Version 3.0
short_description: PKCS#11 v3.0 is the HSM programming interface standard. It adds CKM_HKDF_DERIVE and CKM_HKDF_KEY_GEN mechanisms for TLS 1.3 and CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF / FEEDBACK_KDF / DOUBLE_PIPELINE_KDF for NIST SP 800-108 derivation. Essential for HSM-based QKD integration workflows where QKD secrets are imported as non-extractable keys and used to derive session keys. Superseded by PKCS#11 v3.1 and v3.2.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM key derivation; PKCS#11 CKM_SP800_108_COUNTER_KDF; CKM_HKDF_DERIVE
ToolchainSupport: Thales Luna; Utimaco SecurityServer; nCipher nShield; SoftHSM

document_title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.1 (OASIS Standard)
short_description: PKCS#11 v3.1 adds LMS and HSS stateful hash-based signature mechanisms (CKM_HSS_KEY_PAIR_GEN, CKM_HSS) for quantum-resistant code signing and firmware verification. Also adds EdDSA mechanisms (CKM_EDDSA) and incremental MAC operations. First PKCS#11 version with any PQC algorithm support.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM LMS/HSS stateful signatures; EdDSA; PKCS#11 CKM_HSS
ToolchainSupport: Thales Luna; Utimaco SecurityServer; Entrust nShield; SoftHSM

document_title: PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.2 (Committee Specification Draft)
short_description: PKCS#11 v3.2 adds post-quantum cryptography mechanisms: ML-KEM (CKM_ML_KEM 0x00000017, CKM_ML_KEM_KEY_PAIR_GEN 0x0000000f), ML-DSA (CKM_ML_DSA 0x0000001d, CKM_ML_DSA_KEY_PAIR_GEN 0x0000001c, CKM_HASH_ML_DSA_*), and SLH-DSA (CKM_SLH_DSA, CKM_SLH_DSA_KEY_PAIR_GEN). Introduces C_EncapsulateKey() and C_DecapsulateKey() functions for KEM operations. New key types: CKK_ML_KEM (0x49), CKK_ML_DSA (0x4a), CKK_SLH_DSA (0x4b). New attributes: CKA_PARAMETER_SET, CKA_ENCAPSULATE, CKA_DECAPSULATE, CKA_SEED.
applicable_industries: HSM Vendors; Government; Finance; PKI; Defense
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM PQC key generation; ML-KEM encapsulation/decapsulation; ML-DSA signing; PKCS#11 CKM_ML_KEM; CKM_ML_DSA; C_EncapsulateKey; C_DecapsulateKey

short_description: Defines multi-coin and multi-account HD wallet derivation path structure across blockchains.

short_description: Extends BIP-32 to support Ed25519 and other non-secp256k1 curves for HD derivation; required for Solana.

document_title: EIP-55: Mixed-case Checksum Address Encoding
short_description: Ethereum standard for checksummed mixed-case hex address encoding using Keccak-256 to detect typos.

document_title: SEC 2 v2: Recommended Elliptic Curve Domain Parameters
short_description: SECG standard defining domain parameters for secp256k1 and other elliptic curves used by Bitcoin and Ethereum.

document_title: RFC 8032: Edwards-Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (EdDSA)
short_description: IETF standard for EdDSA including Ed25519 and Ed448. Used by Solana. Deterministic nonce eliminates nonce-reuse vulnerabilities.

document_title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
short_description: Satoshi Nakamoto foundational paper introducing Bitcoin and blockchain: proof-of-work consensus and ECDSA-based ownership.

document_title: Ethereum: A Secure Decentralised Generalised Transaction Ledger
short_description: Formal specification of the Ethereum Virtual Machine by Gavin Wood. Defines Keccak-256 address derivation and ECDSA signing.

document_title: BIP-141: Segregated Witness (Consensus Layer)
short_description: Activates Segregated Witness on Bitcoin separating signature data (witness) from transaction inputs. Introduces SegWit witness versioning (v0 P2WPKH/P2WSH, v1 Taproot). BIP-360 (P2QRH) introduces SegWit v3 (bc1r...) as a quantum-resistant output type building directly on this witness versioning scheme.
authors_or_organization: Eric Lombrozo; Johnson Lau; Pieter Wuille (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin witness versioning; transaction malleability fix; SegWit v0/v1/v3 foundation
ToolchainSupport: Bitcoin Core; Lightning Network; Ledger; Trezor

document_title: BIP-340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1
short_description: Defines 64-byte Schnorr signatures for secp256k1 with provable security, batch verification, and key aggregation (MuSig2). Required foundation for Taproot (BIP-341). BIP-360 (P2QRH) targets Taproot's Schnorr key-spend path as the primary quantum vulnerability to replace with post-quantum signatures.
authors_or_organization: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Tim Ruffing (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin signature scheme; Taproot foundation; quantum exposure surface

document_title: BIP-341: Taproot: SegWit Version 1 Spending Rules
short_description: Activates Taproot on Bitcoin (block 709632). Defines SegWit v1 spending with a Schnorr key-spend path and Merklized Abstract Syntax Tree (MAST) script-spend path. BIP-360 (P2QRH) specifically removes the key-spend path as quantum-vulnerable under Shor's algorithm and proposes a SegWit v3 (bc1r...) replacement.
authors_or_organization: Pieter Wuille; Jonas Nick; Anthony Towns (Bitcoin Core)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Bitcoin Taproot upgrade; Schnorr key-spend quantum vulnerability; BIP-360 context

document_title: Bitcoin BIP-360: Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
short_description: Proposes a new Bitcoin output type (SegWit v3, bc1r...) using post-quantum signatures via a soft fork. Removes the quantum-vulnerable key-spend path from Taproot. Exact PQC algorithms to be standardized in a companion BIP; ML-DSA and FALCON-512 are leading candidates under community discussion. Authored by Hunter Beast to address the HNFL risk to Bitcoin public keys.

document_title: EIP-4337: Account Abstraction Using Alt Mempool
short_description: Enables smart contract wallets on Ethereum without consensus-layer changes. As a PQC migration path, EIP-4337 smart accounts can verify ML-DSA or FALCON signatures on mainnet today — no protocol change required.
authors_or_organization: Vitalik Buterin; Yoav Weiss; Ethereum Foundation
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum wallet; smart account; PQC migration path

document_title: EIP-7702: Set Code for EOA
short_description: Allows EOAs to set executable code via signed authorisation tuples (Pectra hard fork). Provides a bridge for PQC migration: a delegated smart contract can verify ML-DSA or FALCON signatures on behalf of the EOA, enabling quantum-safe signing without full account migration. Note: EIP-7702 authorisation tuples are themselves signed with secp256k1; quantum resistance is provided by the delegated contract's verification logic.
authors_or_organization: Vitalik Buterin; Sam Wilson; Ansgar Dietrichs; Ethereum Foundation
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum EOA; smart account; Pectra hard fork

document_title: Harvest Now Decrypt Later: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Data Privacy Risks for Distributed Ledger Networks
short_description: Federal Reserve FEDS Working Paper (2025-093) analysing HNFL risks for blockchain networks. Examines how quantum computers could retroactively compromise immutable on-chain transaction data across Bitcoin, Ethereum, and distributed ledger systems.

document_title: Tasklist for Post-Quantum Ethereum
short_description: Ethereum Foundation research post outlining the full roadmap to post-quantum Ethereum. Covers STARK-based and lattice-based signature replacement for ECDSA and BLS12-381, and describes the emergency quantum fork path for an unplanned Q-Day.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Ethereum protocol; consensus layer; PQC migration

document_title: Merkle Tree Certificates for TLS (draft-ietf-plants-merkle-tree-certs-01)
short_description: IETF Internet-Draft defining Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs) for TLS, adopted by the IETF PLANTS working group as draft-ietf-plants-merkle-tree-certs-01 (February 2026). Replaces individual per-certificate CA signatures with a single signed Merkle tree root and compact inclusion proofs, reducing PQC TLS handshake authentication data by 63-74%.

document_title: Certificate Transparency Version 2.0
short_description: IETF Standards Track RFC defining Certificate Transparency Version 2.0. Obsoletes RFC 6962. Foundational for understanding the transparency log model that Merkle Tree Certificates extend for post-quantum TLS optimization. Specifies TLS extensions for CT log artifacts and certificate inclusion proofs.

document_title: Keeping the Internet fast and secure: introducing Merkle Tree Certificates
short_description: Cloudflare blog post explaining the Merkle Tree Certificate proposal, its motivation (PQC certificate bloat), architecture (MTCA, transparency service, subscribers), and experimental deployment results. Includes size comparison data.

document_title: Implementation and Analysis of Merkle Tree Certificates for Post-Quantum Secure Authentication in TLS
short_description: Master's thesis from Radboud University providing a practical implementation and performance analysis of Merkle Tree Certificates in TLS 1.3. Evaluates tree construction, proof generation, and handshake overhead with ML-DSA.

document_title: State of the Post-Quantum Internet in 2025
short_description: Cloudflare's annual review of PQC adoption across the Internet, covering ML-KEM deployment in TLS 1.3, browser support (Chrome, Firefox), certificate transparency challenges, and the MTC proposal as a solution to PQ certificate bloat.

document_title: eIDAS 2.0 Regulation (EU 2024/1183)
short_description: EU regulation establishing the European Digital Identity framework mandating EUDI Wallets for all member states by late 2026. Requires private sector acceptance by late 2027. Introduces Qualified Electronic Attestations of Attributes (QEAA) and specifies the trust framework for digital identity across EU. Supersedes eIDAS 1.0.

ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet; OpenID4VCI; OpenID4VP; QES; Digital Identity

short_description: Technical architecture and reference framework for the European Digital Identity Wallet. Specifies PQC migration roadmap: Dec 2026 national roadmaps Dec 2030 high-risk migration Dec 2035 full transition.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: EUDI Wallet; mso_mdoc; SD-JWT VC; OpenID4VCI; OpenID4VP; Trust Framework

document_title: ISO/IEC 18013-5:2021 — Mobile Driving Licence (mDL) Application

document_title: RFC 9901 — SD-JWT-based Verifiable Credentials (SD-JWT VC)

short_description: IETF specification defining the selective disclosure mechanism for JWT claims using salted SHA-256 hashes. document_title: OpenID for Verifiable Presentations (OpenID4VP)
short_description: OpenID Foundation specification defining how holders present verifiable credentials to relying parties. document_title: OAuth Status List — draft-ietf-oauth-status-list
short_description: IETF specification for a compact bit-array status list to check validity states of credentials (valid revoked suspended). Provides privacy-preserving revocation: the verifier cannot determine which specific credential is being checked from the status list position alone. Used in EUDI Wallet ecosystem for credential revocation checking without revealing holder identity to the issuer.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Credential Revocation; EUDI Wallet; SD-JWT VC; Privacy-Preserving Status

short_description: NIST statistical test suite defining 15 statistical tests (Monobit, Runs, Chi-Squared, DFT, Serial, and more) for evaluating randomness quality of bit sequences produced by cryptographic RBGs. Used for validating entropy source output quality prior to NIST ESV submission.

document_title: AIS 20/AIS 31: Functionality Classes and Evaluation Methodology for Deterministic and Physical Random Number Generators

document_title: 2024 Quantum Threat Timeline Report
short_description: Annual expert survey projecting the probability and timeline of a cryptographically-relevant quantum computer (CRQC). Projects 5–15% probability by 2030 and 50% by 2034–2039. Foundational source for Mosca's Theorem and HNDL/HNFL risk calculations.

document_title: Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)
short_description: Core CMS standard defining SignedData, EnvelopedData, and AuthEnvelopedData structures. Foundation for S/MIME email signing and encryption. All PQC CMS RFCs (9629, 9882, 9814, 9708, 9690) extend this format.
applicable_industries: Email; Document Signing; Code Signing; PKI
ProtocolOrToolImpact: S/MIME; CMS; document signing; email encryption

document_title: Using Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) Counter with CBC-MAC (AES-CCM) and AES Galois/Counter Mode (AES-GCM) Algorithms in the Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)
short_description: Defines AuthEnvelopedData content type for AEAD-based encryption in CMS. The authenticated-enveloped structure with AES-GCM provides both confidentiality and integrity — the preferred encryption mode for PQC email migration via RFC 9629.

document_title: Internet Key Exchange Protocol Version 2 (IKEv2)
short_description: Defines IKEv2 for establishing IPsec Security Associations. Foundational protocol for VPN key exchange; all PQC IKEv2 extensions (RFC 9370, draft-ietf-ipsecme-ikev2-mlkem) build on this specification.
applicable_industries: VPN; IPsec; Enterprise; Government; Critical Infrastructure
ToolchainSupport: StrongSwan; Libreswan; Cisco IOS; Juniper Junos

document_title: Use of Curve25519 and Curve448 in the Secure Shell (SSH) Protocol
short_description: Defines curve25519-sha256 and curve448-sha512 key exchange for SSH. The classical KEX baseline that mlkem768x25519-sha256 hybrid extends for post-quantum SSH in OpenSSH 9.9+.

document_title: Rosenpass: Formally Verified Post-Quantum Protocol for WireGuard
short_description: Defines the Rosenpass protocol for adding PQC to WireGuard. Runs a separate ML-KEM-768 + Classic McEliece handshake and injects the combined shared secret as a WireGuard pre-shared key (PSK). Formally verified with ProVerif.
misc_info: WireGuard Protocol

document_title: IEEE 802.1AE-2018: MAC Security (MACsec)
short_description: Defines MACsec — IEEE Layer 2 encryption standard for Ethernet networks. MACsec uses AES-GCM-128/256 for confidentiality and integrity. QKD-derived keys can be injected as Secure Association Keys (SAKs) via the MACsec Key Agreement (MKA) protocol, making MACsec a natural integration point for QKD in enterprise and telecom networks.
ProtocolOrToolImpact: MACsec; Layer 2 encryption; QKD key injection

document_title: Use of Post-Quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms in JOSE
short_description: Defines how to use ML-KEM (FIPS 203) within JOSE/JWE for quantum-safe token encryption. Introduces alg identifiers ML-KEM-512, ML-KEM-768, ML-KEM-1024 for JWE key agreement, replacing quantum-vulnerable ECDH-ES. Companion to draft-ietf-jose-pqc-algorithms for signature migration.

document_title: JSON Web Token Best Current Practices
short_description: Documents security pitfalls and best practices for JWT usage including algorithm confusion attacks, none-algorithm vulnerabilities, audience validation, and expiry enforcement. Essential security hardening baseline before migrating JWT signing algorithms to PQC alternatives.

document_title: JSON Web Token (JWT)
short_description: Defines JWT as a compact URL-safe representation of claims between parties. Specifies the three-part structure (header.payload.signature), registered claim names (iss, sub, aud, exp, iat), and how JWS and JWE are used for signing and encryption. Foundation document for all JWT-based PQC migration work.

document_title: JSON Web Signature (JWS)
short_description: Defines the JWS standard for representing digitally signed content in JSON. Specifies the compact serialization (header.payload.signature) used in every signed JWT and the alg header parameter registry that PQC JOSE drafts extend with ML-DSA and SLH-DSA identifiers.

document_title: JSON Web Encryption (JWE)
short_description: Defines the JWE standard for encrypting arbitrary content using JSON data structures. Specifies the 5-part compact serialization and key agreement mechanisms (ECDH-ES, RSA-OAEP) that ML-KEM replaces in PQC migration. Core reference for JWE encryption and PQC token confidentiality.

document_title: JSON Web Key (JWK)
short_description: Defines the JWK format for representing cryptographic keys as JSON. JWKS endpoints publish public keys for JWT signature verification. PQC migration significantly increases JWKS response sizes: ML-DSA-65 public keys are 1952 bytes vs 65 bytes for P-256, challenging HTTP header limits and caching strategies.

document_title: JSON Web Algorithms (JWA)
short_description: Defines the algorithm registry for JOSE including RS256, ES256, ECDH-ES, AES-GCM, and HMAC. All currently registered signature and key agreement algorithms are quantum-vulnerable to Shor's algorithm. PQC JOSE drafts extend this registry with ML-DSA and ML-KEM algorithm identifiers.

document_title: The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework
short_description: Defines the OAuth 2.0 authorization framework enabling secure delegated access using access tokens. Access tokens and ID tokens issued by authorization servers are typically JWTs signed with RSA or ECDSA. PQC migration requires updating JWT signing across all OAuth authorization servers and resource server token validation.

document_title: OAuth 2.0 Demonstrating Proof of Possession (DPoP)
short_description: Defines DPoP for binding OAuth 2.0 tokens to a client public key using signed proof-of-possession JWTs. DPoP proof JWTs use ECDSA signatures that are quantum-vulnerable. Migration to ML-DSA-signed DPoP proofs requires JOSE PQC standardization and results in significantly larger proof headers per request.

document_title: Recommendation for Key Management: Part 1 – General (Revision 6)
short_description: Major revision of NIST key management guidelines. Adds PQC security categories and quantum-resistant algorithms (FIPS 203/204/205), Ascon (SP 800-232), separates key establishment and key storage discussion, and replaces time-based algorithm approval status. Comment period closed Feb 2026.
applicable_industries: Government; Finance; Healthcare; Defense; Enterprise IT
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Key management policy and HSM deployment

document_title: Key Management Interoperability Protocol (KMIP) Version 2.1
short_description: KMIP v2.1 defines a protocol for communication between key management systems and cryptographic clients. Supports key lifecycle operations (create, locate, get, activate, revoke, destroy) over TLS. Widely implemented by enterprise HSMs (Thales, Utimaco, Entrust) and KMS solutions. Enables interoperability between key management infrastructure during PQC migration.
applicable_industries: Government; Finance; Enterprise IT; HSM Vendors
ProtocolOrToolImpact: HSM and KMS key management interoperability

document_title: Terminology for Constrained-Node Networks
short_description: Defines terminology and device classes (Class 0-2) for constrained-node IoT networks, including RAM/Flash constraints. Foundation for IoT PQC algorithm selection.

document_title: A Firmware Update Architecture for Internet of Things (SUIT)
short_description: Defines the SUIT manifest format for secure firmware updates on constrained IoT devices. Specifies metadata fields used in PQC firmware signing workflows.

document_title: The Datagram Transport Layer Security (DTLS) Protocol Version 1.3
short_description: Specifies DTLS 1.3 — the UDP-based variant of TLS 1.3 used for CoAP and constrained IoT communications. Key reference for PQC handshake overhead analysis on constrained devices.

document_title: TLS Certificate Compression
short_description: Defines certificate compression for TLS 1.3, reducing PQC certificate chain overhead by ~30%. Key mitigation for certificate bloat on constrained IoT devices.

document_title: Using Raw Public Keys in Transport Layer Security (TLS) and DTLS
short_description: Allows TLS/DTLS to use raw public keys instead of X.509 certificates, eliminating certificate chain overhead on ultra-constrained IoT devices. ~70% size reduction.

document_title: Guide to Operational Technology (OT) Security
short_description: NIST guidance for securing industrial control systems (ICS), SCADA, DCS, and OT networks. Covers the Purdue model, network architecture, and security controls for critical infrastructure.

document_title: National Security Memorandum 10 — Promoting U.S. Leadership in Quantum Computing
short_description: US National Security Memorandum directing federal agencies to inventory cryptographic systems and transition to PQC. Established key milestones for quantum-resistant standards adoption.

document_title: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) Key Wrap with Padding Algorithm

Used as CKM_AES_KEY_WRAP_KWP in PKCS#11 v3.2.

document_title: NSA Quantum Key Distribution and Quantum Cryptography
short_description: NSA advisory explaining why it does not recommend QKD for national security systems. States that QKD addresses only the key distribution problem, requires special-purpose equipment, introduces trusted node vulnerabilities, and has side-channel risks in real implementations. document_title: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD); Protocol and Data Format of REST-based Key Delivery API (ETSI GS QKD 014)
short_description: ETSI GS QKD 014 v2.1.1 standardises the REST-based Key Delivery API for Quantum Key Distribution networks. QKD is a separate quantum-safe technology from PQC — it derives keys from quantum-physical principles rather than computational hardness — but is often discussed alongside PQC as a complementary key-establishment option for ultra-high-security and government use cases.
authors_or_organization: ETSI Industry Specification Group on Quantum Key Distribution (ETSI ISG QKD)
SecurityLevels: Information-theoretic security (key establishment only — does not replace authentication or signatures)
ProtocolOrToolImpact: Key delivery REST API; KME-SAE interface
ToolchainSupport: Multiple QKD vendors (Toshiba, ID Quantique, Qubitekk, etc.)
misc_info: QKD complements PQC for high-assurance key exchange but does NOT replace PQC for signatures, authentication, or general-purpose deployment. PQC remains the broader migration target per NSA CNSA 2.0.
